ddls:
CREATE TABLE "connections" (
  "connection_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for each connection */,
  "connection_type_id" INTEGER /* Type of connection (e.g., fiber optic, wireless, satellite) */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the connection (e.g., active, inactive, maintenance) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time the connection was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time the connection was last updated */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the connection */,
  "bandwidth" REAL /* The bandwidth capacity of the connection */,
  "latency" REAL /* The typical latency of the connection in milliseconds */,
  "reliability" REAL /* A measure of the connection's reliability */,
  "last_tested_at" TEXT /* The date and time the connection was last tested */,
  "test_result" TEXT /* The result of the last test */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("connection_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_connections_connection_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("connection_type_id") REFERENCES connection_types ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "connection_sequences" (
  "sequence_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sequence entry */,
  "connection_id" TEXT /* ID of the connection this sequence belongs to */,
  "sequence_number" INTEGER /* Sequence number indicating the order of the connection in the path */,
  "from_node_id" INTEGER /* ID of the node from which the connection originates */,
  "to_node_id" INTEGER /* ID of the node to which the connection is directed */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time the sequence entry was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time the sequence entry was last updated */,
  "distance_km" REAL /* The distance in kilometers between the from_node and to_node */,
  "estimated_latency" REAL /* The estimated latency for this segment of the connection */,
  "last_tested_at" TEXT /* The date and time this segment was last tested */,
  "test_result" TEXT /* The result of the last test for this segment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sequence_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_connection_sequences_to_node_id FOREIGN KEY ("to_node_id") REFERENCES nodes ("node_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_connection_sequences_from_node_id FOREIGN KEY ("from_node_id") REFERENCES nodes ("node_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_connection_sequences_connection_id FOREIGN KEY ("connection_id") REFERENCES connections ("connection_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "nodes" (
  "node_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each node */,
  "node_name" TEXT /* Name of the node */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the node */,
  "type" TEXT /* Type of the node (e.g., router, switch, server) */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the node (e.g., active, inactive, maintenance) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time the node was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time the node was last updated */,
  "capacity" REAL /* The maximum data processing capacity of the node */,
  "current_load" REAL /* The current load on the node */,
  "last_tested_at" TEXT /* The date and time the node was last tested */,
  "test_result" TEXT /* The result of the last test */,
  "maintenance_due_date" TEXT /* The date when the next maintenance is due */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("node_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_nodes_type FOREIGN KEY ("type") REFERENCES node_types ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "connection_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each connection type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the connection type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the connection type */,
  "max_bandwidth" REAL /* The maximum bandwidth capacity for this type of connection */,
  "average_latency" REAL /* The average latency for this type of connection */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "connection_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each version of a connection */,
  "connection_id" TEXT /* ID of the connection this version belongs to */,
  "version_number" INTEGER /* Version number of the connection */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the connection in this version */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the connection in this version */,
  "version_date" TEXT /* Date and time the version was created */,
  "bandwidth" REAL /* The bandwidth capacity of the connection in this version */,
  "latency" REAL /* The typical latency of the connection in this version */,
  "reliability" REAL /* The reliability of the connection in this version */,
  "version_details" TEXT /* Additional details about the changes made in this version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_connection_versions_connection_id FOREIGN KEY ("connection_id") REFERENCES connections ("connection_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "node_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each node type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the node type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the node type */,
  "max_capacity" REAL /* The maximum data processing capacity for this type of node */,
  "average_load" REAL /* The average load for this type of node */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "network_performance" (
  "performance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance metric */,
  "connection_id" TEXT /* ID of the connection this metric belongs to */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the performance metric (e.g., latency, throughput) */,
  "metric_value" REAL /* Value of the performance metric */,
  "metric_unit" TEXT /* Unit of the performance metric (e.g., ms, Mbps) */,
  "metric_date" TEXT /* Date and time the metric was recorded */,
  "test_type" TEXT /* The type of performance test */,
  "test_duration" INTEGER /* The duration of the performance test in seconds */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("performance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_performance_connection_id FOREIGN KEY ("connection_id") REFERENCES connections ("connection_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "maintenance_records" (
  "record_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each maintenance record */,
  "connection_id" TEXT /* ID of the connection this record belongs to */,
  "maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date and time the maintenance was performed */,
  "maintenance_type" TEXT /* Type of maintenance (e.g., routine, emergency) */,
  "details" TEXT /* Details of the maintenance performed */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the maintenance (e.g., completed, in progress) */,
  "next_maintenance_due" TEXT /* The date when the next maintenance is due */,
  "maintenance_window" TEXT /* The maintenance window during which the maintenance can be performed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("record_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_maintenance_records_connection_id FOREIGN KEY ("connection_id") REFERENCES connections ("connection_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, engineer, analyst) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time the user account was last updated */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* The phone number of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* The date and time of the user's last login */,
  "last_logout" TEXT /* The date and time of the user's last logout */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the system */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date and time of the access event */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., login, logout) */,
  "access_ip" TEXT /* IP address of the user */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the device used */,
  "login_attempts" INTEGER /* The number of login attempts made by the user during this session */,
  "session_duration" INTEGER /* The duration of the user's session in seconds */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "incidents" (
  "incident_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each incident */,
  "connection_id" TEXT /* ID of the connection the incident relates to */,
  "reported_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who reported the incident */,
  "reported_at" TEXT /* Date and time the incident was reported */,
  "incident_type" TEXT /* Type of incident (e.g., outage, performance issue) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the incident */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the incident (e.g., open, in progress, resolved) */,
  "resolved_at" TEXT /* Date and time the incident was resolved */,
  "impact_level" TEXT /* The level of impact of the incident */,
  "affected_nodes" TEXT /* A comma-separated list of node IDs affected by the incident */,
  "resolution_summary" TEXT /* A summary of how the incident was resolved */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("incident_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_incidents_connection_id FOREIGN KEY ("connection_id") REFERENCES connections ("connection_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_incidents_reported_by FOREIGN KEY ("reported_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "incident_comments" (
  "comment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each comment */,
  "incident_id" INTEGER /* ID of the incident the comment belongs to */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the comment */,
  "comment_date" TEXT /* Date and time the comment was made */,
  "comment_text" TEXT /* Text of the comment */,
  "attachment_url" TEXT /* A URL to any attachments related to the comment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("comment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_incident_comments_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_incident_comments_incident_id FOREIGN KEY ("incident_id") REFERENCES incidents ("incident_id")
)
